historical_anchor.location: North Atlantic Ocean, approximately 400 miles south of Newfoundland
historical_anchor.fixed_outcome: The RMS Titanic sinks, resulting in the loss of over 1,500 lives.

shared_fact_ledger.name: The Titanic is sinking
shared_fact_ledger.description: The ship has struck an iceberg and is rapidly taking on water, with its bow submerging.

shared_fact_ledger.name: Insufficient Lifeboats
shared_fact_ledger.description: There are only enough lifeboats for approximately half of the people on board, leading to desperate choices.

shared_fact_ledger.name: Class Segregation
shared_fact_ledger.description: Access to lifeboats and certain parts of the ship is heavily influenced by passenger class, with third-class passengers facing significant barriers.

shared_fact_ledger.name: Distress Calls
shared_fact_ledger.description: Wireless operators are sending out CQD/SOS signals, but the nearest assisting ships are hours away.

perspectives.identity: A diligent First Class Steward, accustomed to order and service.
perspectives.tension: Torn between his duty to assist wealthy passengers and his own survival, witnessing the unraveling of social order and the stark realities of class privilege.
perspectives.decision_point: Whether to follow orders to prioritize first-class passengers or help those in greater need, where to seek a lifeboat amidst the chaos.

perspectives.identity: A young, overwhelmed Junior Wireless Operator.
perspectives.tension: Battling technical failures and the sheer volume of distress messages, facing the grim reality that help may not arrive in time, and the isolation of his critical post.
perspectives.decision_point: Which ships to contact, what information to relay, when to abandon the wireless room as the power fails and the ship lists.

perspectives.identity: A young Irish immigrant traveling in Third Class, seeking a new life in America.
perspectives.tension: Confronting physical barriers and class discrimination preventing access to upper decks and lifeboats, fighting for her life and the lives of those she cares about amidst panic and misinformation.
perspectives.decision_point: How to navigate the labyrinthine lower decks, whether to follow rumors or try to force her way to the boat deck, and who to trust for guidance.

period_vocabulary.meaning: Early form of radio communication used for sending and receiving messages over long distances.
A male attendant on a ship, responsible for serving passengers and maintaining cabins.
A dividing wall or barrier within the hull of a ship, often designed to be watertight to contain flooding.
The part of a ship providing the cheapest accommodation for passengers, typically in a shared dormitory setting, synonymous with Third Class.
The international Morse code distress signals used by ships; CQD was an earlier signal, largely superseded by SOS.

synopsis.tone: Desperate, suspenseful, tragic, poignant, historically grounded.

world.location: North Atlantic Ocean, approximately 400 miles south of Newfoundland
world.fixed_outcome: The RMS Titanic sinks, resulting in the loss of over 1,500 lives.

player_roles.name: Frederick 'Fred' Bell
player_roles.description: A diligent First Class Steward, accustomed to order and service.

player_roles.name: Harold 'Harry' Cottam
player_roles.description: A young, overwhelmed Junior Wireless Operator.

player_roles.name: Eliza 'Lizzy' O'Connell
player_roles.description: A young Irish immigrant traveling in Third Class, seeking a new life in America.
